examples.title: Binary + Operator as Member Function
examples.description: Overloading addition operator for a Complex number class
examples.explanation: The left operand c1 is accessed via 'this' pointer. The right operand c2 is passed as parameter. Both private members are accessible directly.

examples.title: Unary ++ Operator as Member Function
examples.explanation: Prefix ++ modifies and returns reference. Postfix ++ takes dummy int parameter to distinguish from prefix, returns old value by value.

examples.title: Compound Assignment Operator as Member
examples.explanation: Returns *this by reference to enable chained assignment operations.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is string length

examples.title: Comparison Operators as Member Functions
examples.explanation: Comparison operators typically return bool. Can be const since they don't modify object.

examples.title: Subscript Operator as Member Function
examples.explanation: Subscript operator must be member function. Provides bounds checking and const overload for const objects.
